sources: Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'if everything is being done in emails, Excels, PDFs' and 'let's see how this procurement process goes just company wide. How are we being engaged?'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Edward offered ROI/value engineering pro bono; Anthony: 'to the extent that would be helpful... we're just not there just yet'
metrics.narrative: Anthony Ribadeneira articulated a clear operational pain on the 5 November 2025 call: Progyny currently runs procurement manually via emails, Excels, and PDFs with no platform in place, and the organization lacks visibility into how the business engages procurement company-wide. He stated they were rolling out an interim procurement process guideline (expected late November 2025) to inform platform scoping and measure organizational adoption before committing to a platform. The quantified business case or ROI has NOT been built — when Edward offered pro bono value engineering, Anthony declined, saying 'we're just not there yet' because they need to see how the interim process performs first. No quantified savings target, cycle-time reduction, or headcount efficiency metric was stated. The gap: no quantified value case on record. The move is to co-build the ROI once the interim process feedback is available and Anthony is ready to advance the business case.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker)
Avoma call 5 November 2025: Anthony asked detailed questions, receptive tone, but deferred next steps ('reach out in early December')
No follow-up call or activity since 5 November 2025 (7.7 months ago)
Next Step (May 2026): 'Anthony is working through... internal budgeting and stakeholders... reach back to you once he has it' — passive, no champion advocacy visible
LastActivityDate 24 February 2026 (122 days ago), no multi-threading
champion.narrative: Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) is the only buyer-side contact engaged and is a developing, not confirmed, champion. On the 5 November 2025 discovery call he was receptive, asked detailed questions about Zycus Merlin AI capabilities, pricing, and implementation, and stated he would 'keep that in mind' regarding Zycus's NDA requirement and that he would reach back out once the interim procurement process provided clarity. However, seven and a half months have passed with no follow-up from Anthony, no introduction to other stakeholders (CFO, IT, Legal), and no champion behaviors (advocacy, internal selling, or multi-threading). He explicitly deferred building an ROI ('we're just not there yet') and said he would reach out when ready, placing Zycus in a passive wait state. The relationship is single-threaded, cooling, and at risk. Anthony is not actively championing Zycus internally; he is a friendly contact, but not a coach or advocate. The gap: no active champion, no multi-threading, and the single thread has gone quiet for 122 days. The move is to re-engage Anthony with a value-add offer (share a use case or ROI template for AI-first procurement) and request an introduction to the CFO or the executive sponsor driving the platform investment.

sources: Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'I have spoken to many vendors in the space. None seem to me like they really have an AI first solution'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: other vendors' AI 'maybe in two or 3 years, but not now'
Salesforce Competitors__c: empty
competition.narrative: No competitors were named on the 5 November 2025 call or in any Salesforce field. Anthony Ribadeneira stated he had 'spoken to many vendors in the space' and that 'none seem to me like they really have an AI first solution', expressing skepticism that other vendors' AI is mature ('maybe in two or 3 years, but not now'). This implies Progyny is running a quiet multi-vendor evaluation, but Anthony did not disclose who else is in the mix. The Salesforce Competitor field is empty. Progyny is a mid-market US healthcare benefits company, and typical competitors in this segment for AI-driven source-to-contract platforms include Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, and Jaggaer. However, asserting any of these without evidence would be fabrication. The gap: competitive landscape is unknown. The move is to ask Anthony directly on re-engagement who else he is evaluating and where Zycus stands relative to the field, positioning Zycus Merlin (Intake, Anna) as the only showtime-ready AI agent solution he described wanting.

sources: Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'if everything is being done in emails, Excels, PDFs, how is that kind of analysis run?'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'let's see how this procurement process goes just company wide. How are we being engaged? What are the needs?'

Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'I had just been here maybe, like, a month and a half or less than that. So early times. Made some good progress... now starting to turn our attention towards a potential platform'
identify_pain.narrative: Anthony Ribadeneira clearly articulated the operational pain on the 5 November 2025 call: Progyny's procurement function is newly established (Anthony had been there less than two months at the time) and the organization has no procurement platform — everything runs manually via emails, Excels, and PDFs. This creates three specific pains: (1) lack of visibility into how the business engages procurement company-wide ('how are we being engaged? what are the needs?'), (2) inability to measure procurement process adoption and cycle times without a system, and (3) no platform to automate or guide routine procurement requests, forcing Anthony's small team to handle every inquiry manually. He stated they were rolling out interim process guidelines to create structure, but without a platform, they cannot scale or measure effectiveness. The pain owner is Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement), and the pain is confirmed and urgent enough that he was exploring a platform investment within his first two months on the job.

sources: Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'I spoke to our legal team, and apparently, they'd rather put NDAs in place once vendors are shortlisted'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Edward: 'That might be an issue for Zycus. We don't normally release pricing... not under an NDA'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'we'll probably use iRequest. So it'd have to... be able to plug into iRequest'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Edward: implementation ~1.25x software, rule of thumb, fixed-bid SOW after scoping session
paper_process.narrative: Anthony Ribadeneira stated on the 5 November 2025 call that Progyny's Legal team prefers to issue NDAs only after vendor shortlisting, not before the RFQ phase, though he acknowledged Zycus could request a special time-bound NDA earlier if needed to share pricing. Edward confirmed Zycus does not release pricing without an NDA, flagging a potential friction point. Anthony also mentioned they use iRequest for contract management, so the procurement platform must integrate via adapter to pull contract data. No InfoSec review process, SOW/MSA mechanics, or procurement channel (direct vs. partner-led) were discussed. Implementation scoping was touched on (Edward quoted ~1.25x software cost as a rule of thumb, to be refined in a scoping session), but no formal scoping session has occurred. The gap: contracting and InfoSec mechanics are undefined, and the NDA process may delay RFQ participation. The move is to proactively offer Progyny Legal a limited-scope NDA template for the RFQ phase and confirm integration requirements for iRequest.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker role) — the only buyer-side contact on the opp
Next Step (May 2026): 'Anthony is working through the possible project with internal budgeting and stakeholders' — implies budget authority sits elsewhere
No CFO, VP Finance, or C-level exec engaged on the 5 November 2025 call or logged in Salesforce activities
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. On the 5 November 2025 call, Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement, the sole buyer-side contact) mentioned he is 'working through a process' and would need to engage 'internal budgeting and stakeholders' (Next Step, May 2026), but he did not name who holds budget authority or CFO/C-level sign-off. The Salesforce contact roster includes Mark Livingston (CFO) and other exec titles at the account level, but none are linked to this opportunity as contact roles, and none appeared on the single buyer call. Budget authority is implied to sit above Anthony (he must work through internal budgeting), but the person is unnamed and unengaged. The gap: Economic Buyer unknown and not in the conversation. The move is to ask Anthony directly who controls the budget for a $100k+ procurement platform investment and secure an introduction.

sources: Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'procurement process should be published relatively soon... we'll review that feedback and use that document as a guide to inform the scoping out of a platform'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'my perspective, that would be great. But I spoke to our legal team, and apparently, they'd rather put NDAs in place once vendors are shortlisted'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'potentially getting a platform up and running early in the New Year'
Next Step (May 2026): 'Anthony is working through the possible project with internal budgeting and stakeholders' — process stalled at budgeting/stakeholder alignment
decision_process.narrative: Anthony Ribadeneira outlined a phased decision process on the 5 November 2025 call: (1) roll out interim procurement process guidelines company-wide (expected late November 2025) to measure engagement and inform platform scoping; (2) review feedback from that interim process; (3) issue an RFQ for a procurement platform, inviting vendors; (4) shortlist vendors; (5) issue NDAs post-shortlist (Legal's preference, though Anthony acknowledged Zycus may need a special time-bound NDA earlier to share pricing); (6) evaluate and negotiate; (7) aim to get a platform 'up and running early in the New Year' (early 2026). He mentioned his manager would be involved in budgeting insights. However, the process has stalled: the RFQ was never issued (as of 26 June 2026, 7.7 months later), and no follow-up has occurred since May 2026 when Anthony was 'working through internal budgeting and stakeholders'. The gap: the decision process is defined in principle but has not been activated, and the approval chain beyond Anthony is unnamed. The move is to re-engage Anthony, confirm the current state of the interim process rollout, and co-author a timeline to restart the RFQ phase.

sources: Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'I would like to take an AI agent first approach... folks that engage in procurements can have most of their questions answered by that AI agent'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: 'I would like to focus on that AI capability if it is showtime ready... that would be probably a differentiator'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony asked about Merlin Intake, Anna (tail-spend agent), and whether Merlin is a 'true agent'
Avoma call 5 November 2025, Anthony: modules for sourcing, procurement orchestration, not pay (they have another system), integration to iRequest for contracts
decision_criteria.narrative: Anthony Ribadeneira stated clear platform requirements on the 5 November 2025 call: (1) AI-first agent capability — he wants an agent that can answer routine procurement questions, guide buyers on vendor selection and pricing benchmarks ('like ChatGPT'), and autonomously handle tail-spend sourcing and negotiation; (2) modules for sourcing, eProcurement, and AI (Merlin); (3) configurability for approval workflows, data capture, and business process tailored to the interim procurement guidelines rolling out; (4) integration via adapters to their existing systems (mentioned iRequest for contracts). He explicitly stated 'AI capability would be a priority for the vendors I shortlist' and 'that would be probably a differentiator'. He also asked whether Merlin is 'showtime ready' and acting as a 'true agent', expressing skepticism that other vendors' AI is mature. However, no formal RFP criteria, scoring rubric, or written evaluation framework has been shared. The criteria are confirmed from the buyer's voice but not formalized. The gap: no structured decision criteria document or RFP rubric. The move is to help Anthony translate his AI-first vision into a formal evaluation framework and position Zycus Merlin (Intake, Anna agents) as the showtime-ready AI solution.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Anthony Ribadeneira is AI Hungry. On the 5 November 2025 call, he explicitly stated he wants to 'take an AI agent first approach' and that AI capability would be 'a priority for the vendors I shortlist' and 'probably a differentiator'. He described a vision for an AI agent that can autonomously handle routine procurement questions, vendor selection, pricing benchmarks, and tail-spend sourcing, mirroring ChatGPT-like natural language interaction. He also asked whether Zycus Merlin is 'showtime ready' and acting as a 'true agent', demonstrating sophisticated AI literacy and urgency. No AIS score is logged in Salesforce, but the buyer's voice signals strong AI appetite. The Merlin positioning (Intake + Anna agents) is well-aligned with this hunger, but the deal stalled before the AI value was demonstrated or validated.

deal_movement.summary: Deal was created and qualified on 5 November 2025 following an initial discovery call the same day. Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement) expressed interest in an AI-first procurement platform and outlined a phased decision process: roll out interim procurement guidelines (late November 2025), gather feedback, then issue an RFQ. Edward Dlugosz was instructed to follow up in early December 2025. No follow-up call occurred. Next Step (dated May 2026, approximately 1-2 months ago) states Anthony is working through internal budgeting and stakeholders and will reach back once clarity emerges. LastActivityDate is 24 February 2026 (122 days ago). No movement since. Deal is cold.

items.name: H. Anthony Ribadeneira
items.risk: Single point of contact; relationship is at risk of going permanently dark if not re-engaged immediately. Anthony deferred to 'internal budgeting and stakeholders' (May 2026) but has not reached back, suggesting he may be blocked internally, the project may be deprioritized, or he has moved forward quietly with another vendor.
items.title: Head of Procurement
items.sentiment: Receptive but passive; has not followed up in 7.7 months. Cooling.

items.detail: Deal has been dark for 122 days with no buyer engagement since 24 February 2026. Anthony Ribadeneira stated in May 2026 he was working through internal budgeting and stakeholders and would reach back, but no contact has occurred. The close date of 31 December 2026 is not credible. The deal is at risk of being lost to inaction, deprioritization, or a quiet competitor close.

No Economic Buyer or C-level sponsor identified or engaged. Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement) is the sole contact and must work through 'internal budgeting and stakeholders' but has not introduced Zycus to the CFO, his manager, or any executive. The deal is vulnerable to an executive-level decision Zycus is not part of.

Single-threaded relationship with Anthony for 7.7 months. No IT, Legal, Finance, or exec stakeholder engaged. If Anthony leaves, is blocked internally, or loses interest, Zycus has no other buyer relationship to fall back on.

NDA friction: Progyny Legal prefers to issue NDAs only after vendor shortlisting, but Zycus requires an NDA before releasing pricing. Anthony acknowledged this and said he would try to arrange a special time-bound NDA for Zycus, but no NDA was executed. This friction may block Zycus from participating in an RFQ if one is issued.

No quantified ROI or business case built. Anthony deferred building an ROI on the 5 November 2025 call, and Zycus did not follow up. Without a financial case, the deal is unsupported for internal budgeting and executive sign-off, especially given the 122-day silence.

items.expected_effect: Make one final high-level attempt to restart the deal at the executive level before disqualifying. If no response, recommend moving the opportunity to the relevant stakeholder (deprioritized/no decision) and reallocating resources.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is cold and showing all indicators of a lost or deprioritized opportunity. Anthony Ribadeneira has not engaged for 122 days (since 24 February 2026), despite stating in May 2026 he would reach back once internal clarity emerged. No demo, no follow-up call, no RFQ issued, no multi-threading, no Economic Buyer engaged, and no forward movement in 7.7 months. The relationship is single-threaded and cooling. The only positive signal is that Anthony was receptive and articulated strong AI appetite on the 5 November 2025 call, but that signal is now 7.7 months stale. The deal requires immediate, high-value re-engagement or it should be disqualified.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 5 November 2025, qualified same day. In Qualified stage for 234 days (7.8 months). LastActivityDate 24 February 2026 (122 days ago). Close date 31 December 2026 (188 days out). No forward movement since the single discovery call on 5 November 2025 (7.7 months ago). Time-in-stage far exceeds enterprise norms for Qualified (typically 30-60 days to Formal Evaluation or disqualify). No verified buyer engagement in 122 days. Forward slip math: at current zero-velocity, the deal will not close by 31 December 2026.
evidence: Only one buyer call on record: discovery call 5 November 2025 (7.7 months ago). No follow-up calls, no demos, no RFQ issued.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal dormant 122 days with zero buyer engagement since February 2026. Single discovery call 7.7 months ago, no follow-up despite buyer promise to reconnect post-May budget review. No champion, no EB, no competitive process—deal has stalled out.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal is dark and single-threaded with no buyer engagement for 122 days. The primary risks are: (1) the relationship has gone cold and Anthony may have quietly moved forward with another vendor or shelved the project; (2) Zycus has no visibility into the Economic Buyer, CFO, or executive sponsor, leaving the deal vulnerable to an executive-level decision Zycus is not part of; (3) the AI differentiation wedge (Merlin as the only showtime-ready agent solution) was articulated but never demonstrated or validated, so Zycus has no proof of value in Progyny's hands.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is unknown. Anthony Ribadeneira stated on the 5 November 2025 call that he had evaluated 'many vendors in the space' but found that 'none seem to have an AI first solution' that is production-ready, giving Zycus a potential differentiation wedge on Merlin AI (Intake and Anna agents). However, he did not name any competitors, and no competitive intel is logged in Salesforce. Without active buyer engagement or follow-up calls, the current competitive standing is unverifiable. The wedge — if the deal were re-engaged — is to position Zycus Merlin as the only showtime-ready AI agent platform that matches Anthony's stated 'AI-first approach' requirement, citing PWC as a reference customer who replaced two incumbents with Zycus for AI capabilities. The risk is that during the 122-day silence, Progyny may have quietly shortlisted or selected another vendor, or the project may have been deprioritized entirely.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus has a strong AI differentiation wedge that was articulated but never activated. On the 5 November 2025 call, Anthony explicitly stated he wants an 'AI-first agent' solution and that other vendors' AI is not production-ready ('maybe in two or 3 years, but not now'), and Edward positioned Zycus Merlin Intake and Anna as showtime-ready agents with PWC as a reference customer. However, Zycus never delivered a Merlin-focused demo, POC, or proof point, and the deal went dark before the AI value was validated in Progyny's hands. The positioning is strong in theory but unproven in practice, leaving Zycus under-positioned despite having the clearest AI fit in the field.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus aligns strongly with Anthony Ribadeneira's stated AI-first vision from the 5 November 2025 call. He articulated a requirement for an AI agent that can answer routine procurement questions, guide buyers on vendor selection and pricing, and autonomously handle tail-spend sourcing — exactly what Zycus Merlin Intake and Anna agents deliver. Edward positioned Merlin Intake (natural language sourcing, RFP question generation) and Anna (autonomous tail-spend negotiation agent) as production-ready, citing PWC as a reference customer. Anthony was receptive and stated AI capability would be a differentiator for shortlisting. However, the fit is theoretical until re-engaged: no demo, no POC, no hands-on validation has occurred. The primary exposure is the NDA friction (Progyny Legal prefers post-shortlist NDAs; Zycus requires one for pricing) and the lack of a quantified ROI, which Anthony deferred. Fit is strong on capability, but activation is stalled.

Anthony Ribadeneira stated in May 2026 he was working through internal budgeting and stakeholders and would reach back, but no contact has occurred. The close date of 31 December 2026 is not credible. The deal is at risk of being lost to inaction, deprioritization, or a quiet competitor close.

deltas.subject: No Economic Buyer or C-level sponsor identified or engaged. Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement) is the sole contact and must work through 'internal budgeting and stakeholders' but has not introduced Zycus to the CFO, his manager, or any executive. The deal is vulnerable to an executive-level decision Zycus is not part of.

deltas.subject: Single-threaded relationship with Anthony for 7.7 months. No IT, Legal, Finance, or exec stakeholder engaged. If Anthony leaves, is blocked internally, or loses interest, Zycus has no other buyer relationship to fall back on.

deltas.subject: NDA friction: Progyny Legal prefers to issue NDAs only after vendor shortlisting, but Zycus requires an NDA before releasing pricing. Anthony acknowledged this and said he would try to arrange a special time-bound NDA for Zycus, but no NDA was executed. This friction may block Zycus from participating in an RFQ if one is issued.

deltas.subject: No quantified ROI or business case built. Anthony deferred building an ROI on the 5 November 2025 call, and Zycus did not follow up. Without a financial case, the deal is unsupported for internal budgeting and executive sign-off, especially given the 122-day silence.

deltas.to: Single-threaded relationship with the relevant stakeholder (Head of Procurement) for 7.7 months with no multi-threading to CFO, IT, Legal, or executive sponsor.
deltas.subject: Single-threaded relationship with the relevant stakeholder (Head of Procurement) for 7.7 months with no multi-threading to CFO, IT, Legal, or executive sponsor. The deal is vulnerable to an executive decision Zycus is not part of. The move is to ask Anthony for an introduction to the budget authority (likely the CFO or his manager) and to IT/InfoSec for integration and security scoping, converting this into a multi-threaded, exec-visible opportunity.

deltas.to: AI differentiation wedge articulated but never validated. Anthony explicitly stated he wants an 'AI-first agent' solution and that other vendors' AI is not prod
deltas.subject: AI differentiation wedge articulated but never validated. Anthony explicitly stated he wants an 'AI-first agent' solution and that other vendors' AI is not production-ready, giving Zycus a clear Merlin positioning advantage. However, Zycus never delivered a Merlin-focused demo, POC, or hands-on proof point during the 5 November 2025 call, and no follow-up occurred. The move is to re-engage Anthony with a live Merlin Intake + Anna demo tailored to Progyny's tail-spend and routine procurement use cases, and offer a 2-week pilot to prove the agent capability he described wanting.

deltas.to: No quantified ROI or business case built, leaving the deal unsupported for internal budgeting and executive sign-off. Anthony deferred building an ROI on the 5
deltas.subject: No quantified ROI or business case built, leaving the deal unsupported for internal budgeting and executive sign-off. Anthony deferred building an ROI on the 5 November 2025 call, saying 'we're just not there yet', and Zycus did not push back or offer a lightweight ROI template. Seven months later, with no follow-up, the deal remains un-anchored to a financial or operational case. The move is to proactively deliver a one-page AI-first procurement ROI model (cost avoidance on tail-spend via Anna, cycle-time reduction, headcount leverage) benchmarked to Progyny's ~$100k investment, and offer to co-build it with Anthony and his finance stakeholder.

However, Zycus never delivered a Merlin-focused demo, POC, or hands-on proof point during the 5 November 2025 call, and no follow-up occurred. The move is to re-engage Anthony with a live Merlin Intake + Anna demo tailored to Progyny's tail-spend and routine procurement use cases, and offer a 2-week pilot to prove the agent capability he described wanting.

gaps: Only 1 transcribed buyer call on record (5 November 2025). One additional meeting (18 November 2025) had no recording, limiting depth of MEDDPICC and competitive read.
No follow-up call, demo, or RFQ participation in 7.7 months — limited ability to assess current buyer state, competitive position, or deal health.
No Economic Buyer, CFO, IT, Legal, or exec stakeholder on any call or contact role — single-threaded coverage.
No AIS score, AIS_Status, or AIS_Why populated in Salesforce — buyer AI appetite inferred from call content only.
Competitors__c empty; competitive landscape unknown (Anthony stated he evaluated 'many vendors' but did not name them).
Next Step (May 2026) is the most recent buyer-state signal, but it is 1-2 months old and not re-confirmed — unclear whether Anthony is still working through budgeting or has moved on.
